location.office_time_required: Monday, Tuesday, and Thursday in-office; Wednesday optional WFH; Friday depends on role/team

level_signals: 8+ years in senior project/program management roles
Leads complex projects across GTM, Finance, Sales, Marketing functions

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: 8+ years in senior project/program management roles with proven experience in leading complex projects for GTM, Finance, Sales, Marketing, and/or enterprise data business functions
Solid understanding of GTM systems:Salesforce CRM, CPQ, Netsuite, Marketo, Segment CDP, Support chatbot and how they support business operations
Balance a program workload and staffing according to project/program and organizational priorities and goals

Have a solid understanding of the SaaS business model and specifically how a customer success, sales, marketing, and finance org functions. Deep understanding of E2E customer lifecycle from discovery, trial, purchase, adopt, and renew.
Experience with CRM platforms, peripheral apps that integrate to it and how these support business processes, as well as customer success and sales applications
Deep experience in driving projects in an agile/scrum, waterfall or hybrid environment.

Demonstrated experience in all phases of both the Software Development Life Cycle (SDLC) and non-technology project lifecycle, including identification of business problems, development of business cases and project plans, and project management and closeout.
You build strong relationships and trust with stakeholders, keeping them and their teams engaged
Ability to manage a complex web of key partnerships across internal organization teams and other GTM and Finance orgs within Asana.
Demonstrates curiosity about AI tools and emerging technologies, with a willingness to learn and leverage them to enhance productivity, collaboration, or decision-making.

scope.surface: Enterprise Technology portfolio — GTM, Finance, Sales, Marketing systems and processes
core_responsibilities: Improve the cohesiveness between Enterprise Technology and stakeholders by evolving methodologies, execution model, communications and utilization of agentic resources
Organize the overall project team and establish clear roles and responsibilities at the beginning of each project; hold the project team accountable for the deliverables
Proactively detect misalignment and risk; gain consensus on appropriate actions to mitigate negative impact
Ensure creation, prioritization and update of the GTM project roadmap and sequence roadmap work to maximize velocity
Manage the full lifecycle of projects and programs from kick-off and planning to execution and closure
Create repeatable project management processes to provide leverage to scale the business

compensation.equity_form: equity mentioned as possible component of compensation package

green_flags: Explicitly encourages applications from candidates who don't meet every requirement
Well-defined scope — GTM, Finance, Sales, Marketing systems and processes
Equity mentioned as part of total compensation
red_flags: Role requires on-site presence in Vancouver office (Mon/Tue/Thu minimum) — eliminates remote candidates
Compensation stated in CAD, not USD — may disadvantage candidates comparing to US-market roles
Visa sponsorship and work authorization requirements are not stated
8+ years minimum is a fairly high bar with no stated flexibility
AI curiosity listed as a must-have unconditional bullet but is essentially a cultural fit signal — severity classification is ambiguous

fields_inferred_not_stated: seniority.level (inferred from 8+ years requirement and compensation range)
scope.business_model (inferred from SaaS context and Asana's product)
scope.primary_metrics_inferred (not stated directly; inferred from responsibilities)
compensation.base_range_usd (not stated; salary given in CAD only)
ambiguities: AI curiosity bullet ('Demonstrates curiosity about AI tools...') appears as an unconditional bullet in the 'About you' section, suggesting hard requirement, but the content is more cultural fit / willingness-to-learn. Classified as hard per schema rules but flagged here.
Friday in-office policy is ambiguous: 'depends on the type of work you do and the teams with which you partner' — not a firm requirement.
Compensation range is in CAD, not USD — USD equivalent not provided and not inferred.
